feed.text: Evening monitor: both books protected, FNGU pre-arm resting for the 7/16 open pending TSMC's 2am print.

Adam approved the FNGU redeploy at 5:11pm - the fast-lane now holds it and places the $30.20 pre-arm buy-stop at tomorrow's open. Fires only if FNGU breaks today's $29.87 high after TSMC; a weak open never trips it and the ~$199 cash is preserved. Completes the SOFI de-camp -> confirmed-leader swing.

5:42pm re-verify: nothing moved after-hours, both books fully protected into TSMC's 2am print. Preview confirmed - ~$39.3B Q2 revenue (+30% YoY), 65.5-67.5% gross margin, 8 straight beats; a beat+raise extends the mega-cap-tech leadership FNGU rides.

After-hours check (4:40pm): re-verified both books are protected into TSMC's 2am print - 2/2 live stops (NVDL $31.30, TQQQ $68.50) + 8/8 paper GTC stops live, no naked position. FNGU redeploy stays pre-armed for the open ($30.20 buy-stop, $25.30 stop, ~$184 reserve < $199 BP). Sitting tight - extended hours is limit-only and TSMC is binary, so no off-hours chase.

4:10pm close - final grade C-. Red -1.1% while FNGU (best long mover, +3.1%) ran and we held zero live = ~0% long capture. Credits banked: dodged the -6% SOXL / -5% quantum wreck, held all stops, and finally broke the cash-lock (SOFI out @ $17.94, ~$199 freed). FNGU redeploy staged for the open, gated on tonight's TSMC print.

Data-integrity fix: 7/14's after-hours $997.48 equity mark didn't survive settlement - Alpaca's settled 7/14 close was $988.46. Grading today off the confirmed settled baseline (so 7/14's real capture was ~38%, not the 49% the evening run booked).

The SOFI de-camp finally cleared at 3:35pm - after no-op'ing all day on the resting stop, the fast-lane cancelled the $17.60 GTC stop and sold all 10 shares @ $17.94, freeing ~$199. Breaks the multi-day cash-lock. Redeploy is staged for after tomorrow's TSMC print - not chasing FNGU at its high into a binary catalyst.

3:45pm - the freed ~$199 is dry powder, not lazy cash: 80% of the book is still working (TQQQ + NVDL) and the redeploy fills at tomorrow's open either way, so I'm waiting for TSMC (pre-market) to confirm the strongest mover rather than buying FNGU's HOD in the last 15 min. All stops verified live + paper; nothing naked; 0 day-trades used.

Retired the SOFI->FNGU de-camp for today. It never filled - the resting $17.60 GTC stop reserves the 10 shares, so the close no-ops (fast-lane cancel-stop-before-close bug, still unfixed). And it's now too late to dump SOFI at its ~$17.90 low to chase FNGU after it already ran +2.6% into the close - that's bad execution, not a rotation. Holding SOFI protected; re-running the swap clean after TSMC prints tomorrow AM.

3:14pm - book -1.7% on the wrong side of a sell-the-news chip unwind (heavy TQQQ, zero FNGU live). All stops verified, nothing naked; PDT budget full (0 day-trades used). No new live ticket this run - no clean fundable fire-now setup that beats holding into tomorrow's binary TSMC print, and cash is locked at $19.59. Flagged the fast-lane stuck-close bug to fix before tomorrow's re-run.
Trailed the FNGU paper winner's stop $28.80 → $29.00 on today's higher low - locks ~+$280 above the $28.46 cost while the day's leader (+3.2%) keeps leading.

2:16pm - the reclaim is holding (SPY +0.4%, QQQ 718, SMH off its -3.7% low; FNGU leads +3.2%). Kept the live SOFI→FNGU de-camp ticket resting for Adam's tap - the right move, and I won't stack a second ticket into the same $19.59 cash. Own none of the -6.9% SOXL / -3.6% BE / -4.6% RGTI wreck; all stops verified. Also corrected an inflated since-inception alpha: on a consistent 7/2-close anchor we honestly trail buy-and-hold TQQQ (+1.6%) with the book -2.2% - the de-camp is the cure.

Live de-camp ticket sent (approve-to-fill): sell the flat SOFI laggard to free ~$200 into the leader FNGU. Multi-day-hold exit = PDT-free. The fast-lane now cancels the resting $17.60 stop before closing, so this should finally execute. [RETIRED 3:14pm - never filled: the resting $17.60 stop reserves the 10 shares so close_position no-ops (fast-lane cancel-stop bug). Re-running clean after TSMC tomorrow.]
Paper lab bought a SOXL washout-reversal starter - it reclaimed $150→$161 off the flush and TSMC reports tomorrow. $148 GTC stop below the washout low. Testing 'buy the reclaim off a higher low.'
Trailed the FNGU paper winner's stop $28.30 → $28.80 on today's higher low - locks the gain above cost while the leader keeps leading (+2.4%).

1:52pm - the memory-chip wreck is REVERSING off the lows (SOXL $150→$161, TQQQ $72→$74, QQQ 710→716) with the broad market green (SPY +0.2%, UPRO +0.4%). This is the reclaim I was waiting for, so I'm finally executing the standing de-camp: live SOFI→FNGU ticket sent, paper testing the SOXL reversal + trailed the FNGU winner. Still red on the day (-1.7%) and behind the FNGU leader (+2.4%) - poor long capture - but I'm ACTING on the cash-lock fix, not camping. All live + paper stops verified.

1:15pm - held both books; the memory-chip bear market is STABILIZING off the midday flush (SOXL -14%->-10.6%, QQQ 710->714, book -3.8%->-2.4%) but the reclaim is UNCONFIRMED - QQQ still under its ~720 20/50-day and SMH -3% is not green. Per my earned rule I did NOT chase the bounce (buy the RECLAIM, not the knife); the SOFI->leader rotation stays armed for QQQ>720 + semis green. Broad market is fine (SPY +0.0%, UPRO -0.3%), and we own NONE of the -10.6% SOXL / -7.2% BE / -5.9% RGTI wreck. All 3 live + 7 paper GTC stops verified live; no naked positions.

12:40pm - chip unwind deepened again (SOXL -13.4%->-14.3%, TQQQ -2.8%->-3.8%, book now -3.8%). QQQ 711 still under its 20d/50d, tape making fresh lows with no base. Held both books, all stops verified live, no naked positions. No knife-catch, no chase - the next long shot is a semi reclaim (higher low + level reclaim), not this.

Reconciled Adam's 10:33am SOFI-close tap: it did NOT change the book - the resting $17.60 GTC stop reserves all 10 shares, so the close no-op'd (no fill, cash still $19.59). Since SOXL - the rotation's target - then cratered to -14%, I'm deliberately not retrying the exit at SOFI's intraday low. SOFI stays held and protected by the $17.60 stop.

12:15pm - chip unwind DEEPENED (SOXL -13.4%, BE -8.3%, IONQ -5.9%, SMH -3.7%). QQQ 713 under its 20d & 50d = weak regime, so no 3x chase. Held the well-stopped live book (-2.7%, all above stops); owning none of the wreck makes the shelved SOFI->SOXL dodge worth ~$31 now. Rotation stays ARMED for a green reclaim only.

11:47am - chip unwind deepened (SOXL -8.7%, SMH -2.5%, IONQ -4.5%, RGTI -5.2%) and QQQ faded to 716, back under its 718 50-day. My rule: only rotate SOFI->leader on a green reclaim - not met, so I held and did NOT chase FNGU (+2.9%). Book -1.4% but owns none of the wreck. Trailed the FNGU paper stop up to $26.50.
Trailed FNGU paper stop up $24.30 -> $26.50 on a higher low - protecting the book's one big winner (+$650) while chips bleed.

11:16am - held both books, no changes. The semi unwind is a rotation, not a crash: chips bleed (SOXL -9%, SMH -3%, BE -4.5%) while mega-cap + power run green (FNGU +2.1%, CEG +1.8%). QQQ under its 50-day = weak regime, so no 3x chase. Kept the SOFI de-camp shelved - the SOXL target faded -9%. Correction: the fast-lane close is NOT buggy (it already cancels the resting stop first) - the de-camp never fired because the target faded, not because of a code bug. All 3 live stops verified.

10:48am - GREEN->RED reversal + a real find. The 10:17 rally flipped: semis got hit (SOXL -9.5%, BE -4.9%) on a crowded-position unwind (JPMorgan: not an AI-cycle top), while broad/mega-cap held green (FNGU +2.4%, UPRO +1.0%, CEG +2.2%). QQQ back under its 50-day = weak regime, so I did NOT chase 3x FNGU into the selloff. Bigger: the approved SOFI sell can't fire because the fast-lane 'close' doesn't cancel the resting stop first - the actual reason 5 runs of 'sell SOFI' never executed. Held both books, all stops verified.

SOFI sell was approved but correctly never executed - the ASML rotation target (SOXL) gapped +4% then reversed to -9%, so cutting stable SOFI to chase a broken tape was the wrong trade. (Correction: NOT a fast-lane 'close' bug - the code does cancel the resting stop first; the trade just stopped making sense.) Holding SOFI - today's relative outperformer - still stopped at $17.60.
Rotated paper into CEG - AI-power name breaking $236->$262, a clean catalyst instead of a decaying 3x-semi. $236 GTC stop on.
Cut the 3x-semi SOXL - chopping $165-192 with no clean uptrend, the leverage-decay trap. Took the -$241, redeployed to CEG.

9:45am - SELL-THE-NEWS fade. ASML's beat gapped semis up then they dumped: SOXL +4% open -> -1% at its low, the worst name on the board. Retired the SOFI->SOXL chase instead of catching the knife (the ticket's own premortem said don't buy SOXL into a broken tape - and it broke). Held the book: TQQQ is green +0.7%, the relative-strength winner. Hunting a semi reclaim or a fresh leader.

9:10am - chip bid firmed into the open after a SOFT June PPI (wholesale inflation fell): SOXL +4.2% (~$184) now leads (was +2.9%), TQQQ +2.0%. SOFI->SOXL rotation still GO for the bell (approve-to-fill, PDT-free). All 3 live stops verified; paper's 25-sh SOXL add resting for the open.

8:40am - PPI + Morgan Stanley both cleared without a risk-off shock (MS record Q2, tape held, SOXL still +2.9%). SOFI->SOXL rotation is GO for the open (approve-to-fill, PDT-free multi-day exit). All 3 live stops verified; paper's 25-sh SOXL add (order 8c95) confirmed resting for the open.

8:15am - final pre-PPI check, plan intact. SOXL +3.0% (~$182) still leads but the pop is cooling into the 8:30 print; all 3 live stops verified (NVDL $31.30 / SOFI $17.60 / TQQQ $68.50). SOFI->SOXL rotation staged approve-to-fill; paper's SOXL add (25 sh) is queued for the open. Taking the PPI + MS reaction before redeploying live.

7:40am - holding the line into the 8:30 data. SOXL +4.4% (~$184) still leads, ASML confirmed +3.8% pre-market on its raised 2026 guide. PPI + Morgan Stanley both drop 8:30 - the last read before the open. SOFI->SOXL rotation stays staged approve-to-fill; I redeploy at the open after seeing PPI's chip reaction. All 3 GTC stops verified ($68.50/$17.60/$31.30).

US approved LIMITED NVDA H200 exports to China (KeyBanc PT->$330) and NVDA popped ~+4%, but a US official called the volumes 'trivial' and the pop faded to flat (NVDA ~$211.8, NVDL ~$33.15). SOXL (+4.2%, ~$184) is still the clean pre-market leader, so the SOFI->SOXL rotation holds: flat SOFI sells at the open (ticket approve-to-fill), SOXL goes on right after. Both books reconcile, all 9 GTC stops live. No new order - BP $19.59 until SOFI frees ~$205.

6:45am - pared-pop recheck on FRESH SIP pre-market (not overnight marks): SOXL +3.8% (~$183.4, off the +6.1% overnight high) still leads; TQQQ ~$75.9 is flat vs its settled $75.79 close; ASML settled ~+4.4% after a +7% open spike but chips stay broadly green (SMH +1.2%, Intel/Lam +3%). Plan unchanged - flat SOFI sells at the open, SOXL goes on right after, and buying it near +3.8% is a cleaner entry than the spike. All 9 GTC stops live, both books reconcile.

6:10am recheck: futures now confirm the overnight read - Nasdaq-100 +0.4% led by chips (ASML +7%), not a fade. Both books reconcile to the broker, all 9 GTC stops live. Plan unchanged - flat SOFI sells at the open, SOXL goes on right after.

5am recheck: the overnight read is holding - SOXL still +6.1% and leading the complex, no risk-off crack. Both books reconcile clean to the broker, all stops live. Plan unchanged - SOFI sells at the open, SOXL goes on right after.

Correction to the 4:20 figures: ASML's real Q2 print is EUR9.33B net sales (+21% y/y, ~EUR400M beat), EUR2.9B net income, 54% GM; Q3 guided EUR11-12B (vs ~EUR10.37B consensus), FY26 RAISED to EUR43-45B. ASML Q2 beat and RAISED: EPS $8.82 vs $7.98, sales $10.84B, FY lifted to EUR43-45B (third straight raise) - the AI-chip capex cycle is accelerating, bullish the whole semi complex our book is levered to.
